title: MATLAB: Working with Functions
summary: This page demonstrates how to plot functions in MATLAB, specifically f(t) = e^(-t)cos(2πt), highlighting the importance of choosing an adequate number of sample points. It shows that too few points (default integer spacing) fails to capture oscillatory behavior, while 100 points per oscillation (step size 0.01) produces an accurate plot. The page references sampling theory as a formal treatment of this question.

figures.caption: Plots of f(t) = e^{-t}cos(2πt) sampled with coarse spacing t = (-2:2) (top) and fine spacing t = (-2:0.01:2) (bottom), illustrating how sampling resolution affects the accuracy of the plotted waveform.
